2606.23595.title: SPIRAL: Learning to Search and Aggregate
2606.23595.paper_summary: Language model reasoning can be substantially improved at test time via scaffolds that scale inference compute across different primitives -- sequential reasoning within a trace, independently sampled parallel traces, and aggregation of multiple reasoning traces into a final response. During post-training, however, language models are optimized only for sequential reasoning within a single trace. We introduce Sequential-Parallel-Aggregative Reinforcement Learning (SPIRAL), a framework in which a language model is trained to use all three primitives, as part of a unified inference compute pipeline. Concretely, the language model first samples a set of independent traces in parallel, each produced through sequential chain-of-thought reasoning, and then generates a final aggregation trace conditioned on those traces; all components are optimized end-to-end against the reward of the final aggregated response. To train this system, SPIRAL uses set reinforcement learning to teach models to produce a set of traces that are collectively useful for an aggregator and standard reinforcement learning to teach models to aggregate the set into improved final responses. Our experiments on reasoning tasks show that SPIRAL effectively scales with inference compute, outperforming GRPO by up to 11$\times$ scaling efficiency and 15% higher performance when all three compute primitives are scaled.
2606.23595.paper_summary_zh: 語言模型的推理可以在測試時透過擴展推理計算來顯著改善，這些推理計算涵蓋不同的原始元素——在一個追蹤內的序列推理、獨立取樣的平行追蹤，以及將多個推理追蹤聚合成最終回應。然而，在後訓練階段，語言模型僅針對單一追蹤內的序列推理進行優化。我們引入了序列-平行-聚合強化學習（SPIRAL），這是一個框架，其中語言模型被訓練使用所有三種原始元素，作為統一推理計算管道的一部分。具體來說，語言模型首先平行取樣一組獨立的追蹤，每個追蹤都是通過序列思考推理產生的，然後生成一個基於這些追蹤的最終聚合追蹤；所有組件都是針對最終聚合回應的獎勵進行端到端的優化。為了訓練這個系統，SPIRAL使用集合強化學習來教導模型產生一組對聚合器集體有用的追蹤，並使用標準強化學習來教導模型將這組追蹤聚合成改進的最終回應。我們在推理任務上的實驗顯示，SPIRAL有效地隨著推理計算的擴展而擴展，超越GRPO的擴展效率高達11$\times$，並在所有三種計算原始元素擴展時性能提高15%。
2606.23595.author: Jubayer Ibn Hamid et.al.
2606.23595.authors: Jubayer Ibn Hamid, Ifdita Hasan Orney, Michael Y. Li, Omar Shaikh, Yoonho Lee, Dorsa Sadigh, Chelsea Finn, Noah Goodman
